underlying_goal: Fix a corrupted Laravel project after mass HTTP→HTTPS replacement, resolve PHPStan errors, restore missing legal consent checkboxes in registration forms, replace abandoned packages, migrate config enums to native PHP enums, and install development tooling — all while establishing strict boundaries on what the assistant can modify without permission.
friction_detail: Assistant repeatedly made unapproved changes to composer.json/phpunit.xml tooling configs, replaced the legal consent block with new content instead of restoring the historical version, and chose wrong package versions causing compatibility issues.
brief_summary: User needed a broad Laravel project cleanup — fixing syntax corruption, restoring missing legal checkboxes, resolving PHPStan errors, replacing abandoned packages, and migrating config enums — and most tasks were completed successfully, though the assistant overstepped on tooling configurations multiple times before boundaries were established.
user_instructions_to_assistant: Do not modify composer.json or tooling configuration without explicit permission
Always show diffs before editing files
Restore the original legal consent block from the historical view file, not create new content
